value_chain_position: Core banking software, payment processing infrastructure, and capital markets technology platform; sells multi-year software licenses, SaaS contracts, and transaction-processing services to global financial institutions.
relative_comps: FIS was the largest financial technology company by revenue post-Worldpay acquisition, but the Worldpay divestiture (2023) reset the business back to a legacy core-banking software provider growing at 3% CAGR. The 14% YoY re-acceleration is partly base-effect driven and partly early Worldpay restructuring benefit. FCF margin of 24% is healthy but the growth profile is closer to a mature infrastructure vendor than a disruption toll-taker. Fiserv and Jack Henry are more focused peers; FIS is the most diversified and arguably the most strategically diluted. The fintech_rails theme partially applies (banking/treasury software is a defined layer) but the s-curve is maturing.
